summary: PR #2320 decompiled or improved two miscellaneous functions in lb audio and menu name-entry code. It replaced the stub for lbAudioAx_80024DC4 with a loop-based audio-pool registration routine, introduced a typed wrapper for lbl_80433710 so existing allocator calls address its alloc field, implemented mnName_8023749C with language-dependent name arrays and a terminator scan, and updated the mnname.h prototype. Automated report evidence confirms lbAudioAx_80024DC4 as a new 100% match and mnName_8023749C as a 97.84% improvement; the PR body claims fuzzy_match_percent 100.0 for all functions, so verification evidence is mixed.

key_files.role: Implemented lbAudioAx_80024DC4 and updated HSD object allocator calls to use lbl_80433710.alloc after lbl_80433710 became a wrapper struct.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lbaudio_ax.c hunks for lbAudioAx_80024DC4, lbAudioAx_ObjFree, lbAudioAx_800263E8, lbAudioAx_8002835C
human_pr_text: summary names lbAudioAx_80024DC4 and describes sound-engine behavior
text_corpus: bot report lists lbAudioAx_80024DC4 as 0.00% to 100.00%

key_files.role: Changed lbl_80433710 from HSD_ObjAllocData to lbAudioAx_PoolAlloc with alloc, x2C, and x70 fields.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lbaudio_ax.static.h typedef lbAudioAx_PoolAlloc and static lbl_80433710 declaration

key_files.role: Implemented mnName_8023749C, added lblanguage include, declared language-specific name arrays, and used scoped opt_common_subs pragma.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: mnname.c include, externs, pragma block, and mnName_8023749C implementation
human_pr_text: summary names mnName_8023749C and describes name-entry behavior
text_corpus: bot report lists mnName_8023749C as 0.00% to 97.84%

key_files.role: Updated mnName_8023749C prototype from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to char* mnName_8023749C(int slot).

what_changed: Replaced the lbAudioAx_80024DC4 stub with code that scans 16 entries of lbl_80433710.x2C for an existing sound id, refreshes the corresponding x70 priority/timer slot to 0xa, otherwise scans for sentinel 0x83D60U and claims that slot.
Recovered a local lbAudioAx_PoolAlloc struct around lbl_80433710 with HSD_ObjAllocData alloc at offset 0x00, x2C at 0x2C, and x70 at 0x70.
Adjusted HSD_ObjFree, HSD_ObjAlloc, and HSD_ObjAllocInit call sites to pass &lbl_80433710.alloc rather than &lbl_80433710.
Added lb/lblanguage.h include and extern declarations for mnNameNew_803EE720 and mnNameNew_803EE724 in mnname.c.

Implemented mnName_8023749C as a language-dependent lookup that selects one of two name arrays, scans until the requested slot or the string-terminator entry, and returns NULL when the selected string starts with the terminator.
Wrapped mnName_8023749C in #pragma push / #pragma opt_common_subs off / #pragma pop.
Updated mnName_8023749C declaration in mnname.h to char* mnName_8023749C(int slot).

smart_moves.text: Recovering lbl_80433710 as a containing struct allowed allocator APIs to keep using the real HSD_ObjAllocData subobject while exposing the adjacent pool arrays needed by lbAudioAx_80024DC4.
The audio-pool implementation uses natural for loops over the fixed 0x10 active channel slots instead of preserving an unstructured branch ladder.
The recovered mnName_8023749C signature was propagated to the owning header instead of leaving UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a static object is both an allocator object and a larger pool record, model it as a containing struct and pass the allocator field to HSD_ObjAlloc/HSD_ObjFree/HSD_ObjAllocInit call sites.
Fixed-size pool scans can often be expressed as simple natural loops over indexed arrays when the repeated logic searches for an existing entry and then a sentinel/free entry.
When a function body proves a signature, update the real header prototype rather than keeping UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: mnName_8023749C prototype changed to char* mnName_8023749C(int slot)

naming_conventions.text: The new containing struct was named lbAudioAx_PoolAlloc, tying the type to the lbAudioAx subsystem and its pool-allocation role; internal unknown arrays remained conservative x2C and x70 offset names.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: typedef struct lbAudioAx_PoolAlloc with x2C and x70 fields

naming_conventions.text: mnName_8023749C kept its address-style global function name while the signature was improved; no unsupported semantic rename was introduced.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used duplicate local pointers p and q both initialized to st->x2C before two separate loops in lbAudioAx_80024DC4, likely to match local register/lifetime shape.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lbAudioAx_80024DC4 declares s32* p and s32* q from st->x2C
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The PR evidence does not include objdiff details explaining why two pointer locals are required.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used a scoped #pragma opt_common_subs off around mnName_8023749C.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: #pragma push, #pragma opt_common_subs off, #pragma pop around mnName_8023749C
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The bot report shows mnName_8023749C improved to 97.84%, not a confirmed exact new match, and no adjacent-function regression evidence is included.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used explicit casts to s8 and u8 in mnName_8023749C terminator and slot comparisons.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: term = (s8) mnName_StringTerminator; loop condition j != (u8) slot; string terminator comparisons
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The slice does not explain whether these casts were required by type recovery or codegen matching.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: GALE01 report showed matched code +140 bytes, one new match for main/melee/lb/lbaudio_ax lbAudioAx_80024DC4 from 0.00% to 100.00%, .bss improvement in lbaudio_ax, and mnName_8023749C improvement from 0.00% to 97.84%.

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR state, author, changed files, merge time
human_pr_text: summary and claimed fuzzy_match_percent 100.0 for all functions
notes: No human review comments or review approvals are present in the loaded slice.

The PR body claims fuzzy_match_percent 100.0 for all functions, but the automated bot report only confirms lbAudioAx_80024DC4 as 100.00%; mnName_8023749C is listed as an unmatched-item improvement to 97.84%.
The slice provides no build log, objdiff screenshot, adjacent-function regression check, or explanation for the opt_common_subs pragma.

The PR body's description of the name-entry function says it appends a string terminator byte, while the shown diff implements a language-dependent lookup returning a char* or NULL; this discrepancy should not be promoted as a confirmed behavior fact without more evidence.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Model allocator-plus-pool statics as containing structs
accepted_candidate_records.text: In lbaudio_ax, PR #2320 recovered lbl_80433710 as a containing lbAudioAx_PoolAlloc with an HSD_ObjAllocData alloc field and adjacent arrays. Existing allocator calls were updated to pass &lbl_80433710.alloc while the matched function accessed pool arrays through the containing type.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Propagate recovered function signatures to headers
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2320 updated mnName_8023749C in mnname.h from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to char* mnName_8023749C(int slot) after implementing the body.

source_update_candidates.title: lbaudio_ax static lbl_80433710 acts as allocator plus audio-pool arrays
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2320 changed lbl_80433710 in src/melee/lb/lbaudio_ax.static.h from HSD_ObjAllocData to lbAudioAx_PoolAlloc, with alloc at 0x00 and arrays x2C and x70 used by lbAudioAx_80024DC4.
source_update_candidates.reason: May be useful as a source-owned path fact for future lbaudio_ax pool and allocator work; should be verified against current source before promotion.

source_update_candidates.title: mnName_8023749C uses language-specific name arrays
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2320 implemented mnName_8023749C by selecting mnNameNew_803EE724 when lbLang_IsSavedLanguageUS() is true and mnNameNew_803EE720 otherwise, then scanning entries until the requested slot or a terminator entry.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential path fact for mn name-entry data usage, but bot report indicates the function was not fully matched in the loaded evidence.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the PR body's claim that fuzzy_match_percent was 100.0 for all functions as a verified match result; the automated report contradicts it for mnName_8023749C.
Do not promote scoped opt_common_subs off as an accepted general tactic from this PR alone; the loaded evidence lacks objdiff rationale and adjacent regression checks.

Do not promote the PR body's name-entry behavior statement about appending a terminator byte as a confirmed description of mnName_8023749C; the diff shows lookup/return logic rather than an append operation.
